Interface SourceElement

SingleLineComment, Singleton, SpecialConstructorReference, SpecialReferenceWrapper, StatementBlock, StatementSVWrapper, Static, StrictFp, StringLiteral, SuperConstructorReference, SuperReference, Switch, Synchronized, SynchronizedBlock, Then, ThisConstructorReference, ThisReference, Throw, Throws, Times, TimesAssignment, TransactionStatement, Transient, Try, TwoState, TypeArgumentDeclaration, TypeCast, TypeDeclaration, TypeOperator, TypeParameterDeclaration, TypeReference, TypeSVWrapper, UncollatedReferenceQualifier, UnsignedShiftRight, UnsignedShiftRightAssignment, VariableDeclaration, VariableReference, VariableSpecification, VisibilityModifier, Volatile, While
public interface SourceElement
A source element is a piece of syntax. It does not necessarily have a semantics, at least none that is machinable, for instance a Comment.

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Interface
    Description
    static class 
    SourceElement.Position
    The position of a source element, given by its line and column number.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    accept(SourceVisitor v)
    Receive a visitor, for instance a pretty printer.
    SourceElement
    deepClone()
    Creates a deep clone of the current source element.
    SourceElement.Position
    getEndPosition()
    Returns the end position of the primary token of this element.
    ProgramFactory

    Get factory.
    SourceElement
    getFirstElement()
    Finds the source element that occurs first in the source.
    SourceElement
    getLastElement()
    Finds the source element that occurs last in the source.
    SourceElement.Position
    getRelativePosition()
    Returns the relative position (number of blank heading lines and columns) of the primary token of this element.
    SourceElement.Position
    getStartPosition()
    Returns the start position of the primary token of this element.
    void
    setEndPosition(SourceElement.Position p)
    Sets the end position of the primary token of this element.
    void

    Sets the relative position (number of blank heading lines and columns) of the primary token of this element.
    void
    setStartPosition(SourceElement.Position p)
    Sets the start position of the primary token of this element.
    String
    toSource()
    Creates a syntactical representation of the source element using the accept(SourceVisitor)method with an internal default pretty printer.

  • Method Details

    • getFirstElement

      SourceElement getFirstElement()
      Finds the source element that occurs first in the source.
      Returns:
      the first source element in the syntactical representation of this element, may be equals to this element.
      See Also:
      • toSource()
      • getStartPosition()

    • getLastElement

      SourceElement getLastElement()
      Finds the source element that occurs last in the source.
      Returns:
      the last source element in the syntactical representation of this element, may be equals to this element.
      See Also:
      • toSource()
      • getEndPosition()

    • getStartPosition

      SourceElement.Position getStartPosition()
      Returns the start position of the primary token of this element. To get the start position of the syntactical first token, call the corresponding method of getFirstElement().
      Returns:
      the start position of the primary token.

    • setStartPosition

      void setStartPosition(SourceElement.Position p)
      Sets the start position of the primary token of this element. To set the start position of the syntactical first token, call the corresponding method of getFirstElement().
      Parameters:
      p - the start position of the primary token.

    • getEndPosition

      SourceElement.Position getEndPosition()
      Returns the end position of the primary token of this element. To get the end position of the syntactical first token, call the corresponding method of getLastElement().
      The end position is inclusive.
      Returns:
      the end position of the primary token.

    • setEndPosition

      void setEndPosition(SourceElement.Position p)
      Sets the end position of the primary token of this element. To set the end position of the syntactical first token, call the corresponding method of getLastElement().
      Parameters:
      p - the end position of the primary token.

    • getRelativePosition

      SourceElement.Position getRelativePosition()
      Returns the relative position (number of blank heading lines and columns) of the primary token of this element. To get the relative position of the syntactical first token, call the corresponding method of getFirstElement().
      Returns:
      the relative position of the primary token.

    • setRelativePosition

      void setRelativePosition(SourceElement.Position p)
      Sets the relative position (number of blank heading lines and columns) of the primary token of this element. To set the relative position of the syntactical first token, call the corresponding method of getFirstElement().
      Parameters:
      p - the relative position of the primary token.

    • getFactory

      ProgramFactory getFactory()
      Get factory.
      Returns:
      the program factory.

    • accept

      void accept(SourceVisitor v)
      Receive a visitor, for instance a pretty printer.
      Parameters:
      v - a source visitor.

    • toSource

      String toSource()
      Creates a syntactical representation of the source element using the accept(SourceVisitor)method with an internal default pretty printer.

    • deepClone

      SourceElement deepClone()
      Creates a deep clone of the current source element. For NonTerminalProgramElements, the parent roles are valid, except that the root element is not included anywhere and hence has no set parents, of course. This method also clones Comment s, but does not clone derived information such as scopes.
